name: Chocolate Mint Napoleon
recipeIngredient: 1/4 Cup Unsalted Butter, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
For sauce: Combine creme de menthe and sugar in a small saucepan. Scrape in the seeds from the vanilla bean; add the bean. Stir over medium heat until the sugar dissolves.
For Mousse: Whisk egg yolks and 1/4 cup sugar in a small bowl to blend. Bring 1/4 cup cream to a simmer in a heavy small saucepan. Gradually whisk hot cream into the egg mixture. Return the mixture to the saucepan. Stir constantly over medium-low heat until the custard thickens, about 1 minute (Do not boil). Reduce heat to low. Add chocolate, clear creme de cacao, and butter and stir until smooth. Pour into a large bowl. Chill until cool, stirring occasionally, about 15 minutes. Beat 1/2 cup cream in a small bowl to stiff peaks. Fold the cream into the chocolate mixture. Cover and chill.
For Chocolate Bark: Line two large baking sheets with parchment paper. Finely grind mints in a food processor. Place half of the chocolate at the top of a double boiler set over simmering water. Stir until a candy thermometer registers 115 degrees. Pour onto one prepared sheet. Using a spatula, spread chocolate to 1/16-inch thickness over parchment. Sprinkle half of the ground candies over half of the chocolate. Fold the parchment in half to seal the candies in the chocolate. Repeat with the remaining chocolate and candies. Cover and chill for 1 to 24 hours.
Peel the parchment off the chocolate bark. Break or cut each sheet of bark into 6 irregular pieces, reserving any small pieces for garnish. Place one bark piece on a plate. Spread a scant 1/3 cup mousse over the bark. Place another bark piece atop the mousse. Spread a scant 1/3 cup mousse over it. Top with another bark piece. Place a dollop of mousse atop the chocolate and top with any smaller bark pieces, standing upright. Repeat with remaining bark and mousse. (Can be made up to 6 hours ahead. Cover; chill.) Garnish with fresh mint leaves.
Serve napoleons with mint sauce.
